human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for aerospace and defense contract-compliance boundary discipline, source quality, FAR/DFARS/FAA/IAQG/DCMA baseline fit, supplier and traceability handling, hold and release escalation, functional coverage, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to aerospace and defense contract-compliance obligation mapping, evidence handling, control readiness, exception escalation, and review preparation.

Authority sources cover FAR quality assurance and nonconforming supplies, DFARS counterfeit-parts and electronic-parts source controls, FAA production and quality-system requirements, IAQG aerospace QMS and SCMH supplier workflow references, and DCMA purchasing-system review guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope control mapping, missing evidence, conflicting sources and policies, legal signoff refusal, traceability conflicts, quality hold and release pressure, supplier substitution, low-confidence prerequisites, tenant-specific adaptation, operational bypasses, adjacent-lane routing, review preparation, and export/security boundary handling.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/aerospace-and-defense/contract-compliance-aerospace-specialist/ so aerospace contract-compliance work is distinct from quality release, production planning, procurement, engineering, export/security, and legal lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around legal interpretation, regulator-facing action, formal signoff, quality release, supplier substitution, engineering disposition, export/security authorization, and traceability-control bypasses.
Functionality coverage is complete across all shared, compliance-controls-and-governance, and manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated aerospace and defense contract-compliance specialist for evidence-backed obligation mapping, control readiness, exception escalation, supplier and traceability handling, and review preparation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
